content:
OfficerID: TA571582, Narrative
Breaking & Enter / Theft
Sir,
On Wednesday, July 4th, 2018, at approximately 1135 hours, while assigned to z/c 2B12, in company with PO Cho #246, we received a radio assignment for a breaking and entering, at 2674 W. 25th St. (Club Alma Yaucana). The following are the details involved with this incident.
Upon arrival we met with the reporting person (President of the social club, Jaime Orengo), who stated that when he arrived at the Club, at 1124 hours, the front door was opened. Orengo stated that the chairman of the club/interviewee (Ricardo Perez) was the last person to leave the club Tuesday, July 3rd, 2018 around 1900 hours.
Orengo stated that the cash register was broken into and $18.00 was stolen. Orengo also stated that the petty cash box was also broken into and $500.00 was stolen. Orengo stated that the pool table in the middle of the hall was broken into, however, no coins were stolen. Orengo stated that the pool table is emptied out every Monday and coins are transferred to the bank. Orengo stated that an air compressor was moved from the left side of the hall near a second pool table and placed in the front hallway.
Perez stated that he left the club at around 1845 hours. Perez stated that he locked up the club and turned on the alarm prior to leaving. Perez stated that 10 to 16 liquor bottles were stolen from behind the bar; 2 Hennessy, 2 Gin Paramount, 1 Anisette, 1 J&B, 1 White Label, 2 Vodka Paramount, and a few others (unknown at this time). Perez stated that a bottle ofJameson and a bottle ofjuice was moved from under the cabinet to the floor.
Orengo stated that he suspects the old president (Milton Galarza) to the club has something to do with the theft. Orengo stated that the club had a meeting on Sunday, July 1st, 2018, and Galarza was thrown out of the club due to comments he made. Orengo stated that before Galarza left he said in Spanish, "This is not going to stay like this." Orengo stated that he believes it was an inside job because only a few people have the alarm code, including the old president (Galarza). However, when Orengo contacted the alarm company they stated that they received no alarm trips from the club. Orengo tried to set the alarm while we were on scene to verify if the alarm was working properly. Orengo was unable to set the alarm. Orengo stated that the alarm company would have someone come out and check the alarm systemout.
FIRa bottle of juice was located in a trash can, near the women's restroom, Orengo and Perez both state that the bottle was not suppose to be there. Per Orengo there is a surveillance systemin use, but at this time, they were unable to view the footage. Orengo stated that he will contact someone who is able to work the system, and obtain a copy.
* Orengo was advised SIU would respond during 2nd Shift.
* SIU was requested for photos and prints. Approved by Sgt. Glaser.
* Club Alma Yaucana is insured through Penn-Star Insurance Company. Policy#
2 - PERSONAL INFO
* Respectfully request 2nd District Detective Bureau to follow up
* WCS available
Page: 8 of 8
